user_prompt:
(i) + (ii) + (iii). Ship the doc + skill and a warn-mode hook on day one. Telemetry tracks outline-hit-rate. Promote to block when
  ≥80% of agent-Reads on large files already had a prior outline call (i.e., the rule is already followed, the block just catches drift). Do not do (iv) —
  substituting Read silently is too invasive; it'll surprise agents and humans both, and the failure mode (agent thinks it has the file but only has a summary) is
   exactly the kind of subtle wrong-state clj-surgeon's hard-refuse principle warns against. and really Where does the outline come from? Re-parse on every call (gt compress structure is already there), or read from a persistent codeindex (already exists in
  internal/codeindex/symbol/)? The codeindex is faster and gives you cross-file dep graph; the on-the-fly parse is always-fresh but slower and per-file only., then Does outline include the dep graph and reverse-call graph, or just the symbol list with line ranges? Clj-surgeon emits both (:ls is symbols, :deps is the
  in-ns adjacency). then What's the LoC threshold for "large file"? Clj-surgeon's win was on a 2768-line file, but the curve probably bends much earlier — at ~300 LoC you've already
  saved tokens
